title: potentilla pensylvanica subsp. hispida
intro: Potentilla pensylvanica subsp. hispida, also known as the prickly cinquefoil, is a subspecies of flowering plant that belongs to the rose family (Rosaceae). The plant is known for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antibacterial properties, which make it useful in treating a variety of health issues. The health benefits of Potentilla pensylvanica subsp. hispida include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate digestive issues, and provide relief from skin conditions such as eczema and acne. The plant can be prepared as a herbal tea, infusion, or tincture, and is often used in traditional medicine to support immune function and overall well-being.
